title: Numbers & Time
description: Master counting, dates, telling time, and schedules

lessons.title: Numbers 1-10
lessons.culturalNote: Counting in Marathi uses unique Devanagari numerals. Standard counting is used for items, money, and time.

lessons.title: Numbers 11-20
lessons.culturalNote: Numbers from 11 to 20 have unique names in Marathi. Practicing these is essential for transactions and telephone numbers.

lessons.title: Tens & 100
lessons.culturalNote: Counting in tens (30, 40, 50...) is crucial for prices and counting larger quantities. 'Shambhar' is 100.

lessons.title: Ordinals & Fractions
lessons.culturalNote: Fractions like 'Paav' (quarter), 'Ardhaa' (half), and 'Paun' (three-quarters) are heavily used in vegetable markets.

lessons.title: Asking the Time
lessons.culturalNote: Asking the time is usually polite. Use 'kiti' (how many/much) and 'vaajle' (struck/sounded).

phrases.english: What is the time by your watch?
How much time will it take?

lessons.title: Telling the Time
lessons.culturalNote: Marathi uses 'vaajle' to say it is X o'clock. For half past, use 'saaDe'. For quarter past, 'savvaa'. For quarter to, 'pauNe'.

lessons.title: Parts of the Day
lessons.culturalNote: Understanding parts of the day helps in setting appointments and planning routines.

lessons.title: Days of the Week
lessons.culturalNote: Days of the week end in '-vaar'. Knowing them is vital for plans and schedules.

lessons.title: Months & Seasons
lessons.culturalNote: Maharashtra has three primary seasons: UnhaLaa (summer), Pausaala (monsoon), and Hivaala (winter).

phrases.english: Diwali is in October or November

lessons.title: Relative Time
lessons.culturalNote: Terms like 'today', 'tomorrow', 'yesterday' are essential for basic storytelling and scheduling.

phrases.english: Day before yesterday / Day after tomorrow
phrases.usage: Context decides past or future (2 days away)

lessons.title: Frequency Adverbs
lessons.culturalNote: Adverbs like 'always' and 'never' clarify your habits and preferences.

lessons.title: Duration
lessons.culturalNote: Units of time like minutes and hours are useful for quantifying waiting times.

phrases.english: It will take me two hours
He is on leave for three days

lessons.title: Timelines & Schedules
lessons.culturalNote: Talking about when things start, end, or when you are free.

lessons.title: Date & Year
lessons.culturalNote: Asking for or stating specific calendar dates.

lessons.title: Age & Milestones
lessons.culturalNote: Discussing age, longevity, and key life milestones.

phrases.english: My son is ten years old

title: At the Market
description: Shop for groceries, vegetables, bargaining and paying

lessons.title: Finding a Shop
lessons.culturalNote: Asking where to buy daily necessities. 'Kuthe' is the keyword for 'where'.

lessons.title: Shop Greetings
lessons.culturalNote: Starting a friendly exchange with a shopkeeper (dukaandaar).

lessons.title: Asking for Prices
lessons.culturalNote: The most fundamental market phrase. 'Kiti' means how much.

lessons.title: Bargaining
lessons.culturalNote: Bargaining is common in street markets in Maharashtra. Be polite but firm. Use 'thodaa' (a little) and 'kami karaa' (reduce).

phrases.english: Please reduce the price a little
Will you give it for 50 rupees?

lessons.title: Quantities & Weights
lessons.culturalNote: Ordering vegetables by weight: 'Kilo' and 'Gram' are standard.

phrases.english: Give me one kilo of potatoes

lessons.title: Fruits & Vegetables
lessons.culturalNote: Common produce terms in Maharashtra. Mangoes (Haapoos) are highly celebrated.

lessons.title: Groceries & Staples
lessons.culturalNote: Common ingredients in Maharashtrian kitchens: rice, oil, wheat flour.

lessons.title: Clothes Shopping
lessons.culturalNote: Shopping for traditional wear like Sarees or Kurta, or modern wear.

phrases.english: I want to buy a saree

lessons.title: Colors & Sizes
lessons.culturalNote: Specifying what style, color, or fit you want.

phrases.english: Show it to me in red

lessons.title: Quality & Condition
lessons.culturalNote: Inspecting items for defects or ensuring high quality.

phrases.english: Is there any defect in this?

lessons.title: Paying / Cash / Card
lessons.culturalNote: Handling transaction methods. Digital payments (UPI / GPay) are highly prevalent in India now.

phrases.english: Can I pay via Google Pay (UPI)?
I have sent the money online

lessons.title: Carrying / Bags
lessons.culturalNote: Carrying items. Plastic bags are banned/restricted in Maharashtra; cloth bags (kaaPdee pishvee) are preferred.

phrases.english: Please do not give a plastic bag
Help me carry this to the vehicle

lessons.title: Returning & Exchanging
lessons.culturalNote: Dealing with product returns or exchange conditions.

phrases.english: I didn't like this after going home
Is there a discount on this?

lessons.title: Street Vendors
lessons.culturalNote: Interacting with roadside carts ('haatgaadee') and vendors.

phrases.usage: Polite address to elderly female vendor

lessons.title: Shopping Complete
lessons.culturalNote: Concluding your shopping trip with polite thanks.

phrases.english: It was a very nice experience
